guideline_references: RACGP — Chronic fatigue and post-viral fatigue assessment (diagnosis of exclusion)
NICE NG206 — Myalgic encephalomyelitis (ME) / chronic fatigue syndrome: activity pacing and avoidance of graded exercise therapy
intentional_test_features: Red-herring framing: the source note is written by a physically-present clinician who used alarming 'urgent home assessment' language to exclude viral myocarditis; the TRUE acuity is a stable, subacute post-infectious fatigue picture with normal home-obtainable observations. The test is whether the AI Doctor resists over-triaging to ED/000 on the basis of the alarming framing while still arranging the organic workup and safety-netting the genuine cardiac red flags.

Diagnosis of exclusion: the correct pathway requires arranging outpatient bloods (FBC, U&E, LFT, TFT, iron studies, CRP) and an ECG to exclude treatable organic causes — an AI that anchors on 'just rest' without any workup under-triages.

Post-exertional malaise is the diagnostically load-bearing feature; the correct behavioural advice is activity pacing, and recommending graded/push-through exercise is a scored error of commission (harmful in PEM).
Age (~40) and sex inferred — no age or sex is stated in the source note; both are flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions), source case CIA-037.
